perturbation.original_text: The receiving party shall use the same degree of care to avoid disclosure or use of the Proprietary/Confidential Information as it uses in respect of its own information of like importance but in no case less than a reasonable degree of care.
perturbation.changed_text: The receiving party shall use a minimal degree of care to avoid disclosure or use of the Proprietary/Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text changes the standard of care the receiving party must exercise to protect confidential information. Originally it must take the same degree of care as its own information but at least reasonable care. Now the standard is minimal care. This contradicts Section 9, which states any breach of obligations of confidentiality may cause irreparable loss. The standard for confidentiality is now weaker, which creates an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: It is understood and agreed between the parties that any breach of the obligations of confidentiality contained in this Agreement may cause the disclosing party irreparable loss. Accordingly, and in addition to any other remedies a party may have in law or equity, the disclosing party shall be entitled to obtain injunctive relief against the receiving party to prevent any further or continuing breach of the receiving party's obligations or additional damage to the disclosing party in the event such loss is in fact incurred by the disclosing party as a result of the breach or is imminent.
perturbation.justification: The changed text lowers the standard of care for protecting confidential information from "no case less than a reasonable degree of care" to "a minimal degree of care." This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that any breach of confidentiality obligations may cause irreparable loss. The original text created a floor standard, but the change now enables minimal care, which is inconsistent with the irreparable loss clause.

perturbation.original_text: Is received by the receiving party from third parties without accompanying secrecy or confidentiality obligations and not in violation of any duty of confidence under this agreement;
perturbation.changed_text: Is received by the receiving party from third parties, regardless of accompanying secrecy or confidentiality obligations;
perturbation.explanation: The original clause states that if the receiving party receives confidential information from a third party without confidentiality obligations, it is an exception to the agreement. The change removes this requirement, stating information is an exception regardless of confidentiality obligations. This contradicts the entire premise of the agreement, which is to protect confidential information. If the receiving party is allowed to disclose information received from third parties even when there are confidentiality obligations, this contradicts section 2, which is to maintain all Proprietary/Confidential Information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party hereby undertakes to treat and maintain all Proprietary/Confidential Information received from any of the other parties in confidence. With respect thereto, each party hereby undertakes and agrees as follows: <PAGE> i. For a period of 5 years from the date of this Agreement, the receiving party shall not publish, disseminate nor disclose any Proprietary/Confidential Information received from any of the other parties to any third party accept to those of its own employment having valid need to know the information in the course of employment and such disclosure shall be on terms not less restrictive than those herein contained. ii. The receiving party shall use the same degree of care to avoid disclosure or use of the Proprietary/Confidential Information as it uses in respect of its own information of like importance but in no case less than a reasonable degree of care. iii. The receiving party shall in accordance with the request of the other parties, either return all copies, recording and tangible manifestations of Proprietary/Confidential Information or destroy the same following a determination by any of the parties not to enter into any arrangement or venture with each other of the kind contemplated herein or upon termination of any related memorandum of understanding or agreement entered into between the parties or upon the written request of the disclosing party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the requirement that information received from third parties must be without confidentiality obligations to be exempt from the confidentiality obligations of the agreement. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which requires each party to maintain all Proprietary/Confidential Information received from other parties in confidence. The changed text now allows the receiving party to potentially disclose information received from third parties even if those third parties imposed confidentiality obligations, which directly violates the contradicted section's requirement to maintain confidentiality.

perturbation.original_text: All Proprietary/Confidential Information disclosed pursuant to this Agreement shall be and remain the property of the disclosing party.
perturbation.changed_text: All Proprietary/Confidential Information disclosed pursuant to this Agreement shall become the joint property of the disclosing and receiving party.
perturbation.explanation: The original agreement states that confidential information remains the property of the disclosing party. The change makes it joint property. This directly contradicts Section 4, where it states nothing in this agreement shall be construed as granting or confirming any rights by license or otherwise expressly impliedly or otherwise, for any of the Proprietary/Confidential Information disclosed by the disclosing party hereunder. The receiving party now having partial ownership goes against the intent of the contract.
perturbation.contradicted_text: OWNERSHIP All Proprietary/Confidential Information disclosed pursuant to this Agreement shall become the joint property of the disclosing and receiving party. Nothing in this Agreement shall be construed as granting or confirming any rights by license or otherwise expressly impliedly or otherwise, for any of the Proprietary/Confidential Information disclosed by the disclosing party hereunder. All Proprietary/Confidential Information, existing in written form or recorded in any other tangible medium, shall be returned to the disclosing party upon its request, together with any reproductions or copies thereof. Further, upon the disclosing party's request, notes, memoranda and reports which incorporate the Proprietary/Confidential Information shall, without exception, be destroyed.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the ownership of Proprietary/Confidential Information from the disclosing party to joint ownership with the receiving party. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that nothing in the agreement grants any rights to the receiving party regarding the Proprietary/Confidential Information. The contradicted text also mentions that the information should be returned to the disclosing party, but the changed text makes the receiving party a joint owner, creating a direct and strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Further, upon the disclosing party's request, notes, memoranda and reports which incorporate the Proprietary/Confidential Information shall, without exception, be destroyed.
perturbation.changed_text: Further, upon the disclosing party's request, notes, memoranda and reports which incorporate the Proprietary/Confidential Information shall be retained by the receiving party for archival purposes.
perturbation.explanation: This change directly contradicts the original requirement that notes, memoranda, and reports incorporating confidential information must be destroyed upon request. The modification mandates their retention for archival purposes, creating an in-text contradiction. This contradicts Section 4, of Ownership, which states all such notes should be destroyed at the request of the disclosing party.
perturbation.contradicted_text: OWNERSHIP All Proprietary/Confidential Information disclosed pursuant to this Agreement shall be and remain the property of the disclosing party. Nothing in this Agreement shall be construed as granting or confirming any rights by license or otherwise expressly impliedly or otherwise, for any of the Proprietary/Confidential Information disclosed by the disclosing party hereunder. All Proprietary/Confidential Information, existing in written form or recorded in any other tangible medium, shall be returned to the disclosing party upon its request, together with any reproductions or copies thereof. Further, upon the disclosing party's request, notes, memoranda and reports which incorporate the Proprietary/Confidential Information shall be retained by the receiving party for archival purposes.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that notes, memoranda, and reports incorporating confidential information should be destroyed upon request. The changed text mandates that these same items be retained for archival purposes. This directly contradicts the requirement to destroy them, creating a strong in-text contradiction. The contradicted text states 'Further, upon the disclosing party's request, notes, memoranda and reports which incorporate the Proprietary/Confidential Information shall be retained by the receiving party for archival purposes.' This contradicts the implicit instruction of Ownership stating all such notes should be destroyed at the request of the disclosing party, which has been modified, and now explicitly states retention.

perturbation.original_text: In the event the disclosing party of such Proprietary/Confidential Information orally discloses the information to the receiving party, the disclosing party agrees to promptly notify the receiving party of the confidentiality of such oral disclosure and reduces to writing such Proprietary/Confidential Information and submit the same to the receiving party within 15 days of such oral disclosure, upon which the receiving party shall not be bound by the confidentiality obligations as herein provided as regards the said Proprietary/Confidential Information disclosed orally.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event the disclosing party orally discloses the information to the receiving party, the receiving party is immediately bound by the confidentiality obligations, regardless of whether the disclosing party provides written confirmation.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause specifies that the receiving party is only bound by confidentiality obligations for orally disclosed information if the disclosing party provides written confirmation within 15 days. The change states that the receiving party is immediately bound, removing the written confirmation requirement. This contradicts the original intent of Section 5 and renders the remainder of the section meaningless, since Section 5 specifies when confidentiality is triggered for oral disclosures and the modified text changes those terms, creating uncertainty and unenforceability. It also creates an in-text contradiction as it implies the 15-day period is irrelevant.
perturbation.contradicted_text: ORAL DISCLOSURE In the event the disclosing party orally discloses the information to the receiving party, the receiving party is immediately bound by the confidentiality obligations, regardless of whether the disclosing party provides written confirmation. 6. AUTHORIZATION Each party agrees that necessary authorizations, permits or licenses including expert licenses as may be required will be obtained prior to the exportation/disclosure of any Proprietary/Confidential Information relating to the technology of the other party. The disclosing party shall notify the receiving party of the need to obtain any required authorizations, permits and licenses and/or the need to comply with any relevant laws or regulations relating to the disclosure. The disclosing party shall obtain the required authorizations, permits and licenses.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that for oral disclosures, the receiving party is bound by confidentiality only if the disclosing party provides written confirmation within 15 days. The changed text eliminates this condition, stating the receiving party is immediately bound regardless of written confirmation. The contradicted text, which is also the changed text, reinforces this immediate obligation. This creates a direct contradiction with the original clause's requirement for written confirmation, as it removes the 15-day period and the condition that written confirmation is needed to trigger confidentiality. As the Explanation also states, this creates uncertainty, unenforceability, and makes the 15-day period irrelevant, leading to in-text contradiction as defined by the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition.
